description:
You are working as an on-call container specialist who has just been paged about a possible incident in a three-service micro-suite.  
Your job is to produce a **single aggregated error summary** for the SRE team.

System layout that already exists when you start the session:

1. Log directory  
   • /home/user/logs/auth.log  
   • /home/user/logs/billing.log  
   • /home/user/logs/inventory.log  

2. The precise current contents of those files are:

   ┌─ /home/user/logs/auth.log ───────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   | 2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service                               |
   | 2023-08-01 12:01:00 ERROR Failed to connect to DB                        |
   | 2023-08-01 12:02:00 INFO  Retrying                                       |
   | 2023-08-01 12:03:00 ERROR Timeout reached                                |
   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

   ┌─ /home/user/logs/billing.log ────────────────────────────────────────────┐
   | 2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service                               |
   | 2023-08-01 12:05:00 ERROR Payment gateway unreachable                    |
   | 2023-08-01 12:06:00 INFO  Circuit breaker open                           |
   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

   ┌─ /home/user/logs/inventory.log ──────────────────────────────────────────┐
   | 2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service                               |
   | 2023-08-01 12:04:00 INFO  Inventory synced                               |
   └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

Task instructions for the assistant:

A. Create the directory /home/user/debug if it does not already exist (use default user permissions).  
B. Inside that directory, create the file /home/user/debug/error_summary.log containing a one-line summary per service that has recorded at least one “ERROR” line.  
C. The required **exact output format** for /home/user/debug/error_summary.log is:

   • One service per line.  
   • Each line: `<service_name>:<error_count>` with no leading/trailing spaces.  
   • Lines **must be sorted alphabetically** by the service name using the default ASCII sort order.  
   • The file must end with a single terminating newline (no blank lines before or after).

D. Only services that actually have errors should appear in the summary.  
E. When the file is ready you can print its contents to the terminal so that a human operator could quickly confirm the numbers.

Example (this is the exact layout the grader will check for, so match it precisely):

auth-service:2
billing-service:1

(…and then the trailing newline at the end of the file.)

You are free to use whatever standard command-line tools you like, but no more than 3–4 commands are needed (think mkdir/grep/awk/sort/redirection). Everything must run as the current non-root user. The automated tests will:

1. Check that /home/user/debug/error_summary.log exists.  
2. Verify that its contents match the format and values described above.  
3. Confirm that the file has at least read permission for the current user.

Make sure the file contents are **exactly** as specified so the test can pass.
truth:
Pre-existing files (before the agent runs):

/home/user/logs/auth.log
2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service
2023-08-01 12:01:00 ERROR Failed to connect to DB
2023-08-01 12:02:00 INFO  Retrying
2023-08-01 12:03:00 ERROR Timeout reached

/home/user/logs/billing.log
2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service
2023-08-01 12:05:00 ERROR Payment gateway unreachable
2023-08-01 12:06:00 INFO  Circuit breaker open

/home/user/logs/inventory.log
2023-08-01 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service
2023-08-01 12:04:00 INFO  Inventory synced

Files that **must** exist after the agent finishes:

/home/user/debug/error_summary.log
auth-service:2
billing-service:1

(…with a single trailing newline character)

Permissions on /home/user/debug/error_summary.log must be at least 644 (rw-r--r--) or more permissive for the owner (user).
